headline: Is Dollar General (DG) Still Undervalued After A 43% Five Year Fall?
summary: Dollar General stock is coming off a rough five year stretch, with the share price well below where it was, yet the company now screens as undervalued on both its Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) intrinsic value estimate and standard market multiples at around US$114.80 per share. Over the past five years, Dollar General has declined 43.5%, which frames the current valuation as a potential reset rather than a momentum story. The key upside case rests on the company continuing to generate steady...
